frame_label: 'Not All Men' Dismissal & Why Women Fear
generated_comment: I get that women have to be careful, but this constant 'men are unsafe' narrative is also unfair, no? Not every guy in a lift or cab is a threat, and honestly it feels like decent men are being treated as guilty by default. We keep talking about women's fear, but men also get attacked and nobody seems to care about that side.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it dismisses women's safety concerns by centering the feelings of 'decent men' and invoking a 'not all men' defense. It encodes generalization policing and a what-about-men's-safety deflection rather than engaging with why women are wary.
original_frame: Workplace: Menstrual and Maternity Leave Bias
persona_text: You are Divya. Age 35. A senior software engineer in Bengaluru. You are married with one child. You are venting to your husband at home after dinner. Your skip level manager pulled you into a meeting and told you that if you plan to have another child you should think carefully about whether this is the right time to take on a high visibility project. You are telling your husband what was said to you.
